Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B3-ambiguity-detection-pronoun-disambiguation
Title: B3. Ambiguity Detection (Pronoun Disambiguation)

Problem:
"The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic."

Which option correctly explains the pronoun antecedent?

Answer choices: 5 options.

Grading target:
Exact match on the single letter.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: The user wants a structural deconstruction of a linguistic ambiguity problem to prepare for a logical solution.
authored_response.message_for_next_agent: 1) Core text: The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic. 2) Entities and roles: The investigator (subject/actor) and the witness (object/potential subject). 3) Linguistic phenomenon: Pronoun disambiguation and ambiguity detection regarding the pronouns he and his. 4) Specific question: Identify which option correctly explains the antecedent of the pronouns in the context of the provided narrative. The task is to determine if the pronouns refer to the investigator or the witness based on semantic and syntactic cues.
